confirmed.note: 6 near-identical publish* methods (publishNodeJoin/NodeLeave/ConnectionLost/ConnectionRestored/NetworkSplit/NetworkMerged): same DynamicJsonDocument envelope build, differ only in event_type string and one detail key — classic partial-edited block copy
generateDeviceId() and getISO8601Timestamp() helpers byte-identical across both new files added in the same commit (snprintf ALT-%012X + millis-based fake ISO8601); should have been a shared header

false_positives.what_happened: Message Queue public API is ~8 consecutive thin forwarding wrappers, each opening with the identical guard `if (!messageQueueEnabled) { return 0; }` then delegating to messageQueue.<call>(...). A token/line clone detector clusters these on the repeated guard + delegation skeleton, but it is the legitimate facade/forwarding idiom (one-to-one method exposure of an embedded member), not duplicated logic.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: add a 'forwarding-wrapper' suppressor — if a candidate block's non-boilerplate body is a single `return member.method(args);` / `member.method(args);` delegation (optionally preceded by one early-return guard), and the only token diff between siblings is the callee method name, drop the match. Equivalently raise the min distinct-statement count so blocks whose unique content is <=2 statements after stripping a shared guard don't qualify.

false_positives.what_happened: Internet-health-check API on Mesh is a row of 2-3 line facade methods (setInternetCheckTarget/Interval/Timeout/configure...) each just calling internetHealthChecker.set*(...). Same facade pattern as the message-queue API; siblings look like near-clones because the wrapper shell is fixed and only the delegated setter name varies.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: same forwarding-wrapper heuristic as above. Also useful: treat a sequence of sibling methods that all delegate to the *same* member object as a generated-facade cluster and report it once (or not at all) rather than as N pairwise clones, to avoid quadratic FP inflation on facade classes.

cycle.desc: no cyclic SCC: archcheck --graph reports nodes=83 edges=210 sccs_cyclic=0 largest_scc=1
cycle.evidence: include graph over src/ (painlessmesh/*.hpp, arduino/wifi.hpp, boost/asynctcp.hpp) is acyclic; header layering is strictly downward (wifi.hpp -> mesh.hpp/router.hpp/message_queue.hpp -> protocol/layout/connection). No back-edges. Vendored catch.hpp lives under test/include/catch2 and is excluded from authored-cycle concern.
